vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1312: Finish the Effect migration — migrate all callers to *Effect siblings, then delete the Promise variants from src/lib/

plan.title: Finish the Effect migration — migrate all callers to *Effect siblings, then delete the Promise variants from src/lib/
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1249 made src/lib/ additively Effect-native: every Promise function got an *Effect sibling, but the Promise originals (*Async) were kept to avoid cascade breakage. The result is a permanently half-migrated tree — sync / *Async / *Effect triplets of the same operation, ~60 src/lib/-internal *Async callers, and dead Effect.runPromise bridges. Discovery for this plan also found that PAN-1249 never reached three modules at all: src/lib/agents.ts (verified: zero *Effect exports), src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts, and src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts still expose *Async functions with NO Effect sibling. 'Finishing' the migration therefore means first creating the missing siblings, then migrating every caller, then deleting the Promise variants and canonicalizing the naming so src/lib/ exposes only Effect-native functions.
narratives.Proposal: One feature branch, dispatched as a dependency-wave swarm. Wave 1a (foundation) creates the missing Effect siblings in agents.ts, review-status-db.ts and dag.ts. Wave 1b/1c/1d migrate ALL callers — src/cli/, src/dashboard/ and ~60 src/lib/-internal files — additively onto the Effect siblings; the *Async variants stay in place so every Wave 1 bead is independently mergeable. Wave 1e gates on a clean grep + full quality gates. Wave 2a then deletes the *Async exports and dead runPromise bridges per module in parallel (safe — no caller imports them anymore). Wave 2b runs a single coordinated repo-wide rename pass to canonicalize naming (*Effect/*AsyncEffect become unsuffixed; surviving sync variants become *Sync). Wave 2c verifies the whole migration with grep proofs and quality gates. src/lib/memory/checkpoint-client.ts is explicitly out of scope (its *Async functions have no Effect sibling and no sync variant, and no cli/dashboard caller) — a follow-up issue should migrate it.

autoDecisions.summary: agents.ts / review-status-db.ts / vbrief/dag.ts *Async functions have no Effect sibling; the plan adds Wave 1a to create them before any caller migrates.
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified directly: agents.ts has zero *Effect exports. Callers cannot migrate to a sibling that does not exist, and the *Async variants cannot be deleted. The issue title says 'finish the migration' — completing what PAN-1249 skipped is in scope. Not a contradiction between authoritative inputs, so no escalation; recorded as a discovered scope gap.

autoDecisions.summary: Wave 1 migrates lib-internal callers too, not just src/cli/ and src/dashboard/.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue's Wave 1 table listed only cli + dashboard, but ~60 src/lib/-internal files import *Async variants. The deletion acceptance criterion ('all *Async wrappers ... are deleted') cannot pass while lib-internal callers still import them. The issue heading 'migrate all callers' covers them; the scope was already corrected once for src/dashboard/server/.

autoDecisions.summary: Wave 2 split into 2a (parallel per-module deletion) and 2b (single atomic canonicalization sweep).
autoDecisions.rationale: After Wave 1 no caller imports any *Async, so deleting them is pure dead-export removal touching only each module's own file — parallel-safe. Canonicalization renames exported symbols, which cross-couples every importer; doing it per-module in parallel would conflict, so it is one coordinated final bead.

Its five *Async functions have no *Effect sibling and no sync variant — they are not part of the sync/*Async/*Effect collision the issue targets, and the deletion AC is scoped to '*Async ... that have an *Effect sibling'. They have no cli/dashboard importer. Migrating that client is a separate concern; recommend a follow-up issue.

autoDecisions.summary: CLI migration scoped to the three files importing *Async (scope.ts, agent-status.ts, flywheel.ts) with a per-handler Effect.runPromise boundary.
autoDecisions.rationale: The acceptance criteria require only that no src/cli/ file imports an *Async variant. A wholesale rewrite to a single top-level commander-boundary runPromise across all ~80 commands is not required and would risk repo-wide regression; the existing code already runs Effects at the handler boundary (e.g. close.ts).

items.title: Wave 1a — create Effect siblings for src/lib/agents.ts *Async functions

narrative.Action: src/lib/agents.ts currently exports zero *Effect functions. Create an Effect-returning sibling for every *Async export consumed by callers — getAgentStateEffect, saveAgentStateEffect, setAgentPausedEffect, clearAgentPausedEffect, clearAgentTroubledEffect, recordAgentFailureEffect, getAgentRuntimeStateEffect, listRunningAgentsEffect, stopAgentEffect. Each sibling must be behaviorally identical to its *Async counterpart, use typed errors consistent with src/lib/errors.ts and the *Effect patterns used elsewhere in src/lib/, and compose cleanly (no Effect.runPromise inside). This is additive — do NOT change or delete the *Async functions. existsSync fast stat checks remain acceptable; data-reading FS calls must be async. This bead is the foundation for every agents-consuming caller-migration bead, so its signatures and error channels must be settled before they start.
narrative.Notes: deliverAgentMessage / deliverAgentPermissionDecision (the Channels delivery primitive) call getAgentStateAsync — their migration happens later, but design the getAgentStateEffect error channel so the tmux-fallback policy can still be expressed.

subItems.title: Every *Async export in src/lib/agents.ts has a corresponding Effect-returning sibling
New siblings use typed errors consistent with src/lib/errors.ts and contain no Effect.runPromise
The *Async functions in agents.ts are unchanged; npm run typecheck passes

items.title: Wave 1a — create Effect siblings for src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts *Async functions

narrative.Action: src/lib/database/review-status-db.ts exports upsertReviewStatusAsync and getReviewStatusFromDbAsync with no Effect sibling. Create upsertReviewStatusEffect and getReviewStatusFromDbEffect — behaviorally identical, typed errors (DatabaseError), additive. Do not change the *Async functions.

subItems.title: review-status-db.ts exports Effect siblings for upsertReviewStatusAsync and getReviewStatusFromDbAsync
*Async functions unchanged; npm run typecheck passes

items.title: Wave 1a — create Effect siblings for src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts *Async writer-lock helpers

narrative.Action: src/lib/vbrief/dag.ts exports *Async helpers that back the single-writer plan-file lock — writePlanFileAtomicAsync, applyTaskOperationToPlanFileAsync, removeStaleLockAsync, writePipelineMirrorToPlanFileAsync, readPlanFileAsync, assertSingleWriterAsync, releasePlanWriterAsync — with no Effect sibling. Create Effect-returning siblings, additive, preserving writer-lock acquisition, stale-lock recovery and atomic-write semantics EXACTLY. This is a PAN concurrency invariant — do not relax it.

subItems.title: Every *Async writer-lock helper in vbrief/dag.ts has a corresponding Effect sibling
Single-writer-lock and atomic-write semantics preserved; vbrief dag-writer-lock tests pass
*Async functions unchanged; npm run typecheck passes

items.title: Wave 1a gate — confirm every in-scope *Async function has an Effect sibling

narrative.Action: Build a documented mapping: for every *Async export across the 15 in-scope src/lib/ modules (all 16 *Async-exporting modules except memory/checkpoint-client.ts), record its Effect sibling — a pre-existing *Effect or *AsyncEffect export, a plain-named Effect function with a deprecated *Async alias (pan-dir/specs.ts, pan-dir/continue.ts), or one newly created in Wave 1a. Verify npm run typecheck and npm run build pass. This mapping is the lookup table every Wave 1 caller-migration bead uses.

subItems.title: Every in-scope *Async function maps to a known Effect sibling in a documented mapping
checkpoint-client.ts *Async functions are explicitly recorded as out of scope
npm run typecheck and npm run build pass

items.title: Wave 1b — migrate src/cli/commands/scope.ts off *Async

narrative.Action: scope.ts imports listVBriefsAsync and readVBriefDocumentAsync from lib/vbrief/vbrief-index. Convert the collectLifecycleRows / list-command body to consume listVBriefsEffect and readVBriefDocumentEffect via an Effect.gen pipeline, run it with a single Effect.runPromise at the command-handler boundary, and map typed failures to the existing console output and process.exit codes. Additive — the *Async exports stay.

subItems.title: src/cli/commands/scope.ts imports no *Async variant from src/lib/
pan scope/list command output, error messages and exit codes are unchanged

items.title: Wave 1b — migrate src/cli/commands/agent-status.ts off *Async

narrative.Action: agent-status.ts imports listRunningAgentsAsync (lib/agents), listSessionsAsync and capturePaneAsync (lib/tmux). Migrate to the Effect siblings (listRunningAgentsEffect from Wave 1a, listSessionsAsyncEffect, capturePaneAsyncEffect), composing them in an Effect pipeline run via a single Effect.runPromise at the handler boundary. Preserve the current output.

subItems.title: src/cli/commands/agent-status.ts imports no *Async variant from src/lib/
pan agent-status output is unchanged

items.title: Wave 1b — migrate src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts and its test off *Async

narrative.Action: flywheel.ts imports stopAgentAsync (lib/agents) and sessionExistsAsync (lib/tmux). Migrate to stopAgentEffect (Wave 1a) and sessionExistsAsyncEffect, run via Effect.runPromise at the handler boundary. Update the colocated test src/cli/commands/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts to match (update any *Async mocks/spies to the Effect siblings).

items.title: Wave 2b — canonicalize naming: Effect variants become unsuffixed, sync survivors become *Sync

narrative.Action: One coordinated repo-wide rename pass, run after ALL Wave 2a deletions. For each naming family in src/lib/, rename the Effect variant — currently *Effect or *AsyncEffect (e.g. readPlanEffect, capturePaneAsyncEffect, loadConfigAsyncEffect, getReviewStatusAsyncEffect) — to the unsuffixed canonical name. Where the canonical name still collides with a synchronous variant, keep the sync variant ONLY if a call site genuinely requires synchronous execution and rename it with an explicit *Sync suffix; delete sync variants that have no remaining caller. Update every importer across src/cli, src/dashboard and src/lib in the same pass. This is deliberately a single bead because renaming exported symbols cross-couples all importers and cannot be parallelized per-module.

subItems.title: No src/lib/ export carries an *Effect or *AsyncEffect suffix — the Effect variant is the canonical unsuffixed name
Surviving sync variants carry an explicit *Sync suffix; sync variants with no remaining caller are deleted
All importers updated; npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass

Run the full quality gates and spot-check representative CLI commands and dashboard endpoints for behavior parity. Record checkpoint-client.ts as the only intentional residual (out of scope, follow-up issue).
